title: securetrace – Development Guide
text: R package observability tracing LLM agent workflows. Structured traces spans, token/cost accounting, latency monitoring, JSONL export.

title: securetrace – Development Guide
code: Trace (R6) -- root container for a full agent run   Span (R6) -- single operation (LLM call, tool use, guardrail check)     Event (S3) -- discrete point-in-time within a span     Metrics -- token counts, latency, custom values   Cost -- model pricing tables + calculation   Exporter (S3) -- JSONL, console, multi-exporter   Context -- trace/span stack via module-level environment   Integration -- auto-instrumentation for ellmer/securer/secureguard

title: securetrace – Development Guide
text: R/trace.R – Trace R6 class R/span.R – Span R6 class R/event.R – trace_event() S3 constructor R/metrics.R – record_tokens(), record_latency(), record_metric() R/cost.R – model_costs(), calculate_cost(), add_model_cost() R/exporter.R – jsonl_exporter(), console_exporter(), multi_exporter() R/context.R – with_trace(), with_span(), current_trace(), current_span() R/integration.R – trace_llm_call(), trace_tool_call(), trace_guardrail(), trace_execution()

title: securetrace – Development Guide
text: test-trace.R – Trace lifecycle, serialization, summary test-span.R – Span lifecycle, tokens, events, metrics test-event.R – Event creation, S3 class test-metrics.R – Convenience metric recording test-cost.R – Model pricing, cost calculation test-exporter.R – JSONL, console, multi-exporter test-context.R – Trace/span context stacking, cleanup test-integration.R – Integration wrapper functions

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: securetrace provides observability tracing R-based LLM agent workflows. tracks structured traces spans, records token cost accounting, monitors latency, exports data JSONL analysis.

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: Create trace spans track agent workflow:
code: library(securetrace)  # Wrap your workflow in a trace result <- with_trace("my-agent-run", {    # Each operation gets its own span   with_span("llm-call", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1500, 300, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "The answer is 42"   }) })

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: securetrace includes built-pricing popular LLM models:
code: # View known model pricing (per 1M tokens) model_costs()  # Calculate cost for a specific call calculate_cost("claude-opus-4-6", input_tokens = 5000, output_tokens = 1000)  # Register custom model pricing add_model_cost("my-fine-tuned-model", input_price = 5, output_price = 20)

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: Export completed traces JSONL files console:
code: # JSONL file export exp <- jsonl_exporter("traces.jsonl")  with_trace("exported-run", exporter = exp, {   with_span("work", type = "tool", {     42   }) })  # Console export for debugging debug_exp <- console_exporter(verbose = TRUE)  # Combine multiple exporters both <- multi_exporter(exp, debug_exp)  # Or set a default exporter for all traces set_default_exporter(exp)

title: Getting Started with securetrace
code: # Wrap tool calls result <- with_trace("tools", {   trace_tool_call("calculator", function(x) x * 2, 21) })  # Wrap guardrail checks with_trace("guarded", {   trace_guardrail("length-check", function(x) nchar(x) < 1000, user_input) })

title: Getting Started with securetrace
text: Spans can nested represent hierarchical operations:
code: with_trace("complex-workflow", {   with_span("planning", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(2000, 500, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")      with_span("tool-use", type = "tool", {       # Tool execution within the planning step       42     })   })    with_span("summarize", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1000, 200, model = "claude-haiku-4-5")     "Summary complete"   }) })

title: Getting Started with securetrace
code: tr <- Trace$new("manual-trace") tr$start() s <- Span$new("llm", type = "llm") s$start() s$set_tokens(input = 5000, output = 1000) s$set_model("claude-opus-4-6") s$end() tr$add_span(s) tr$end()  tr$summary() # Trace: manual-trace (completed) #   Duration: 0.01s #   Spans: 1 #   Tokens: 5000 input, 1000 output #   Cost: $0.150000

title: Authors and Citation

text: [!CAUTION] Alpha software. package part broader effort Ian Flores Siaca develop proper AI infrastructure R ecosystem. active development used production official release published. APIs may change without notice. Observability tracing R LLM agent workflows. Structured traces spans, token/cost accounting, latency monitoring, JSONL export.

title: Observability and Tracing for R LLM Agent Workflows
Observability and Tracing for R LLM Agent Workflows
code: library(securetrace)  result <- with_trace("my-agent", exporter = jsonl_exporter("traces.jsonl"), {   with_span("llm-call", type = "llm", {     record_tokens(1500, 300, model = "claude-sonnet-4-5")     "The answer is 42"   }) })

title: Observability and Tracing for R LLM Agent Workflows
text: Structured traces – OpenTelemetry-inspired traces spans R Token accounting – Track input/output tokens per span Cost calculation – Built-pricing Claude GPT models JSONL export – Write traces structured log files Context management – Automatic trace/span stacking with_trace() / with_span() Integration helpers – Wrappers LLM calls, tool executions, guardrail checks

title: Observability and Tracing for R LLM Agent Workflows
Span Class — Span
text: Represents single operation within trace, LLM call, tool execution, guardrail check, custom operation.

title: Span Class — Span
text: name Name span. span_id Unique identifier span. type Type operation: "llm", "tool", "guardrail", "custom". parent_id ID parent span, . metadata Arbitrary metadata attached span. status Current status: "running", "ok", "error". input_tokens Number input tokens recorded. output_tokens Number output tokens recorded. model Model name used span (LLM).

title: Span Class — Span
text: Span$new() Span$start() Span$end() Span$add_event() Span$set_tokens() Span$set_model() Span$set_error() Span$duration() Span$add_metric() Span$to_list() Span$clone()

title: Span Class — Span
code: Span$new(   name,   type = c("llm", "tool", "guardrail", "custom"),   parent_id = NULL,   metadata = list() )

title: Span Class — Span
text: name Name span. type Type operation. One "llm", "tool", "guardrail", "custom". parent_id Optional parent span ID. metadata Optional named list metadata.

title: Span Class — Span
text: input Number input tokens. output Number output tokens.

title: Span Class — Span
text: name Metric name. value Metric value. unit Optional unit string.

title: Trace Class — Trace
text: Root container full agent run. trace contains multiple spans representing individual operations like LLM calls, tool executions, guardrail checks.

title: Trace Class — Trace
text: name Name trace. trace_id Unique identifier trace. metadata Arbitrary metadata attached trace. status Current status: "running", "completed", "error".

title: Trace Class — Trace
text: Trace$new() Trace$start() Trace$end() Trace$add_span() Trace$duration() Trace$to_list() Trace$summary() Trace$clone()

title: Trace Class — Trace
text: name Name trace. metadata Optional named list metadata.
